title: StcCharacter
description: A Save-the-Cat character — one authored character record per design/save-the-cat-sketch-02.md S9. Deliberately minimal (no character-character relations, no per-character arcs at this iteration). `role_labels` is canonical-plus-open per S10 — the verifier recognizes ten canonical labels but the schema admits any non-empty string, so per-genre archetypes and author-introduced labels are admissible. Cross-dialect role-label audit is PFS7 OQ3.

id.description: Stable identifier for this character (e.g., 'C_macbeth', 'C_sheppard'). No prefix convention enforced — the 'C_' convention is descriptive only. PFS7-CH2.

name.description: Display name for this character (e.g., 'Macbeth', 'Dr. James Sheppard'). PFS7-CH2.

description.description: Optional author prose describing this character. Python default is an empty string; schema admits the empty string and absence interchangeably. PFS7-CH3.

role_labels.description: Optional array of role labels. The verifier's one-protagonist advisory and archetype-coverage checks recognize ten canonical labels (protagonist, antagonist, love-interest, mentor, confidant, ally, narrator, victim, suspect, threshold-guardian per save-the-cat-sketch-02 S10); open strings are admissible — the schema admits any non-empty string per S10's canonical-plus-open posture. Tuple-ordering is preserved to accommodate overlapping-role cases (e.g., Sheppard's ('protagonist', 'antagonist', 'narrator')). PFS7-CH4.

authored_by.description: Optional provenance string. Python default 'author'; schema admits the field unconditionally per PFS6-C5-style isomorphic dump. PFS7-CH.
